name: India
summary: Day 63 Thursday — APR 30 IST OPENS INTO WORST-POSSIBLE-SETUP: BRENT +$5 SINCE PREV INDIAN CLOSE, DXY FIRMER +0.4%, US 10Y +7BPS TO 4.42%, FOMC HAWKISH-SKEWED DISSENT REMOVES DXY-RELIEF VECTOR. Apr 29 IST close (last printed): Nifty 24,177.65 (+0.76%), Sensex 77,496.36 (+0.79%), Bank Nifty 55,403.60 (FLAT) — back inside 24,000-24,200 deployment zone on UAE-OPEC bullish-for-India narrative + Q4 FY26 earnings strength. APR 30 IST OPEN AT 9:15 IST FACES: Brent $118-120 close (+6-8% Apr 29 close, Day 1 of $115-sustained CONFIRMED — every $1/bbl Brent = ~$1.5B India annual import bill); FOMC hawkish-skewed dissent (3:1 hawkish-vs-dovish; Powell 'energy inflation hasn't even peaked yet'); DXY firmed +0.4% to 98.99 breaks 98.7 ceiling; US 10Y +7bps to 4.42% (highest 3M); Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First. Mag 7 beat-and-raise PARTIALLY offsetting (positive cue for IT services); Apple tonight (iPhone-supplier cue for Tata Electronics, Wistron India, Foxconn India). USD/INR ₹94.82 (Apr 29 close, Day 3 of ₹94.50 breach) — ₹95.00 trigger ₹0.18 away. RBI selective dollar-sales managing volatility not defending fixed level. Indonesian rupiah record low + USD/KRW +1.0% Apr 29 = parallel EM-Asia stress NOT idiosyncratic. KEY LEVELS APR 30 IST: Nifty 24,200 break-above confirms re-validation; below 24,000 confirms zone breach. Bank Nifty 55,400 strength if holds. USD/INR ₹95.00 → if RBI does not defend, EM-FX cascade probability rises sharply. India 10Y 7.00% → if breaks, FII debt outflow signal. Apr 29 +0.76% Nifty close looks vulnerable on Apr 30 open unless RBI signals (typically don't pre-open). FII April MTD selling ₹56,364 Cr (heavy); FY26 cumulative ₹1.75 lakh Cr. India 10Y GOI ~6.97% likely tests 7.00% on cascade. UAE OPEC exit takes effect tomorrow May 1 — UAE bilateral oil supply scaling to India/China/South Korea/Japan; PSU refiners IOC/BPCL/HPCL already ADNOC mega-refining partners. Brent +$120 Apr 29 close + UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity 12-24mo (medium-term-bearish-for-Brent) creates split-temporal narrative for India: near-term import-bill pressure + medium-term bilateral relief. Chabahar exit ongoing; OMC special-credit-facility (RBI dollar-rationing) at Day 17-18 of 30-day forced-choice window. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): UAE OPEC exit Tue Apr 28 interpreted as BULLISH FOR INDIA — Khaleej Times analyst: 'From India's point of view, this is very good development.' UAE actively pursuing bilateral oil supply with India/China/South Korea/Japan post-OPEC; PSU refiners IOC/BPCL/HPCL already partner ADNOC on mega-refining projects; energy-import-cost concerns partially mitigated. Combined with Q4 FY26 earnings strength (auto/FMCG/realty led; 14/16 Nifty sectors green; Bajaj Finance reporting Wed) and RBI ECL framework clarification settling banking stress (Bank Nifty FLAT after Tuesday's -1.5%), Indian equities rallied: Nifty 24,177.65 (+0.76%, +180 pts), Sensex 77,496.36 (+0.79%) — BACK INSIDE deployment zone (24,000-24,200) after Tuesday's 23,995.70 breach. CRITICAL: this is technical bounce + earnings-driven relief, NOT crisis-resolution signal. Close above 24,200 holding into Thursday confirms re-validation; close below 24,000 confirms breach is structural. Bank Nifty 55,403.60 FLAT print is the cleaner signal — RBI ECL composite catalyst was specific (regulatory clarification), not regime-wide stress. USD/INR closed ₹94.73 Wed (-0.2% rupee weaker, intraday low ₹94.80) — Day 2 of ₹94.50 breach holding; ₹95.00 round-number trigger now ₹0.27 above spot. Business Standard: 'RBI intervention cushions rupee as oil strain brings record low into sight.' RBI stance: SLOW-THE-DECLINE, NOT defend-a-fixed-level. Indonesian rupiah record low Wed = parallel EM-Asia oil-sensitive currency stress (NOT idiosyncratic India). Brent breached $115 intraday Wed (close $114.64 +3.04%) — every $1/bbl Brent = ~$1.5B India import bill annual; structural pressure persists through UAE-bilateral-oil-positive offset. FII April MTD selling ₹56,364 Cr (very heavy); FY26 cumulative ₹1.75 lakh Cr; DII buying Tue ₹1,712 Cr offsetting partial. India 10Y GOI ~6.97% (Apr 27 read); RBI ₹32,000 Cr borrowing this week. Chabahar exit ongoing: US sanctions waiver expired April 26; OMC special-credit-facility (RBI dollar-rationing) at Day 16 of 30-day forced-choice window. FOMC tonight 22:30 IST — Powell's framing of energy inflation 'transitory' vs 'structural' is highest-information-density variable for INR/India tomorrow. CRITICAL CORRECTIONS: yesterday's $100.47/-4.6% Brent Globex print was a yfinance contract-roll glitch. Live tape Mon $108.23 (+2.75%) / Tue $109.39 (+1.08%) — oil REVERSED UP, not down. India's import-bill arithmetic does NOT improve; structural pressure intact. NIFTY 50 closed Monday Apr 27 at 24,092.70 (+0.81%, +194.75 pts), Sensex 77,303.63 (+0.83%, +639.42 pts), snapping a 3-session losing streak — yesterday's deployment-window-entry zone (24,000-24,200) was correctly hit, BUT on Iran's Hormuz-First proposal catalyst (delivered via Pakistan), NOT on Russia-mediator architecture. Tuesday Apr 28 pre-market: GIFT Nifty signaled NEGATIVE open at 24,048 (-72 points) on Trump dissatisfaction with Iran proposal. The 24,000 zone is holding for the WRONG reason: holding despite continued FII selling, on Indian retail/DII flows. FPIs withdrew ₹48,213 cr from cash equity in first 10 days of April per NSDL (FY26 cumulative ~₹1.04 lakh crore). USD/INR ₹94.17 Tue / RBI ref ₹94.1875 — Day 5 of consecutive declines, down ~1.3% on the week (rupee STRENGTHENING marginally). RBI is selling dollars intermittently to slow the slide, not defending a fixed level. India forex reserves $703B as of Apr 17 (vs $728.5B Feb peak) — cumulative ~$25B drawdown. CHABAHAR EXIT: US sanctions waiver expired April 26; India considering full IPGCFZ divest to Iranian entity (per WION, TRT World, Logical Indian). India transferred ~$120M financial commitment before US re-imposed sanctions Sept 2025. The 'Iran-rupee oil track via Chabahar restart' escape hatch is closing — narrows OMC 30-day forced-choice now to: (a) absorb fiscal subsidy ~₹2,800 crore/week, (b) pump-price pass-through, (c) Brent normalization (low-prob in current regime). OMC special-credit-facility (RBI dollar-rationing for IOC/HPCL/BPCL) operational since ~Apr 13-15; 30-day clock now Day 15-16. Goldman raised late-2026 Brent forecast to $90 from $80; Goldman estimates Iran has curtailed up to 2.5M bpd. India is no longer the cleanest Branch 3 beneficiary — operative band shifted UP, not down. Sunday Globex/Monday Asia tape: Brent gap-down to $100.47 → India CAD pressure releases → import bill arithmetic improves immediately (every $5 Brent move = ~$8B annual import bill change) → rupee structural pressure releases → RBI managed-depreciation regime gets breathing room → FII outflow deceleration crystallises (Elara Capital Apr 24: first +$106M inflow in 7 weeks) → Nifty OMC ₹20/litre petrol + ₹100/litre diesel margin compression unwinds → broader Nifty cap-weighted rebound. Monday cash-open probabilities (Branch 3 base case ~60%): Nifty 23,897 → 24,000-24,200 area on +0.5-1.5% rebound; USD/INR ₹94.22 → ₹93.80-94.10 strengthening band; India 10Y 6.87% → 6.80-6.85% area; OMC stocks IOC/BPCL/HPCL likely +1.5-3% on diesel margin recovery. RBI ASKED OMCs TO USE CRISIS-ERA SPECIAL CREDIT FACILITIES [CONFIRMED]: per Reuters via MarketScreener India and ORF, RBI instructed state-run oil refiners (IOC, HPCL, BPCL — ~50% of India's 5.2M bpd refining) to curb spot dollar buying and use special credit facilities for dollar needs — 'crisis-era measures' in place ~2 weeks. Second confirmed RBI crisis-era measure of war (after cumulative ~$5B+ FX reserve drawdowns earlier April). GOVERNMENT FORCED CHOICE WITHIN 30 DAYS: (a) raise pump prices (politically costly); (b) absorb OMC losses via fiscal subsidy (~₹2,800 crore/week); (c) restart Iran-rupee oil track via Chabahar despite US pressure; (d) wait for ceasefire/Brent normalisation. PPAC publishing daily international crude rates = preparation for fuel-price pass-through. Indian press has correctly identified diplomatic theatre is theatre and operational story is fuel-pricing arithmetic + RBI special-credit-facility activation + INR ₹94.22.

current.price: Nifty 23,897.95 / Sensex 76,664.21 / USD-INR ₹94.22 / India 10Y 6.98% (Friday Apr 24 close; Sunday script reproduces)
current.monday_levels: Nifty 23,775 = systematic algo-selling activation; USD/INR ₹94.50 = RBI re-intervention trigger; Nifty 23,500 = contrarian entry; Brent $108 above = Branch 1, $103-107 = Branch 2, below $103 = Branch 3

signals.content: Nifty futures dropped 1.7% on first trading day. Indian equity markets declined ~4% within two days. Goldman Sachs warned India would be 'most impacted in Asia' due to energy import dependency.
Government authorized 58 special evacuation flights to Gulf. Air India, Air India Express, IndiGo leading operations. 1,221 Indian airline services cancelled since Feb 28 due to airspace closures.
52,000+ Indians evacuated from Gulf in first week (Mar 1-7). MEA activated 24x7 control room. Rupee breached ₹92/USD for first time. LPG price hiked ₹60/cylinder.
Two Indian nationals killed in drone strike on Sohar, Oman (Al Awahi Industrial Area). 10 Indians injured. First Indian deaths on land. Three Indian sailors previously killed on merchant vessels.
Another Indian killed in Iranian attack on Riyadh. Total Indian death toll: 6. Rupee hit record ₹93.797/USD. Goldman forecast ₹95. FIIs sold ₹52,704 crore in March.
PM Modi chaired CCS meeting. Reviewed crude oil, gas, petroleum, power, fertilizer supply. Directed whole-of-government approach. Group of Ministers formed. 3 lakh+ Indians returned from Gulf.
Sensex crashed 1,837 points (-2.5%), Nifty fell to 22,513 — lowest since April 2025. ₹14T market cap wiped. PM Modi addressed Lok Sabha on energy security. India imports ~60% of LPG from Gulf.

Nifty bounced +1.8% to 22,912 and Sensex recovered +1.9% to 74,068 — technical bounce from oversold levels with DII buying. But India's March PMI slowed to lowest since October 2022, showing real economy effects materializing beyond markets (CNBC). IPO market frozen. FPIs sold ₹82,700 crore in March alone — largest monthly outflow since October 2024. India quietly approaching China for emergency urea supply while publicly denying any shortage (BigStory Network). Indian tankers Shivalik and Nanda Devi passed through Hormuz safely after Jaishankar-Araghchi diplomatic channel established — India walking a tightrope maintaining ties with Iran for Hormuz access. All-party meeting called for Wednesday on crisis response. Grocery prices rising as crude surged 50%+. Fertilizer plants at 70% capacity due to LNG feedstock cuts.

Government slashed petrol duty from 13 rupees to 3 rupees per liter and completely removed 10-rupee diesel duty on March 27 — most aggressive fuel intervention since 2022. Petroleum Minister Puri said forced to choose between increasing prices or taking fiscal hit. Estimated annual cost $15-20B. (Source: Al Jazeera, confirmed)

Rupee hit intraday low of 94.85/USD on March 27, closed at 94.81 — down 4%+ since war began. FPIs pulled 88,180 crore rupees ($11.5B) in March; 2026 outflows crossed 1 trillion rupees. Forex reserves dropped $7B to $709.75B. RBI net short dollar position approaching $100B. Bond yields at 2-year high. Nifty at 22,820 (-2.1%), Sensex at 73,583 (-2.2%). (Source: Business Standard, RBI data)

TRIGGER ACTIVATED: Rupee breached ₹95/USD (RBI close 95.09 on March 27). Forex reserves dropped $30.14 billion in 3 weeks of March to $698.35 billion as of March 20 (below $700 billion, down from ATH $728.49B on Feb 27). RBI imposed emergency NOP-INR $100 million hard cap on all authorized dealer banks (March 28) — escalation from conventional intervention (selling dollars) to regulatory intervention (restricting bank positions). Manufacturing PMI crashed to 53.8 from 56.9 — weakest since September 2021, input costs rising at fastest pace in 45 months. India in worst position of any major economy: import-dependent for energy, FII-dependent for equity liquidity, burning fiscal and monetary buffers simultaneously. (Sources: Business Standard, RBI data, NSDL, S&P Global PMI)

Monday open: Nifty fell -0.9% to 22,612 despite rupee rally — market sees through mechanical NOP unwinding to underlying oil/FPI pressure. Sensex -0.7% to 73,035. Rupee at ₹93.88 (yfinance, Monday). Banks pushing back on NOP compliance timeline — requesting 3 months vs April 10 deadline. (Sources: yfinance, Business Standard)

EVENING UPDATE: FY26 ended on worst note — Nifty crashed to 22,331 (-2.14%), Sensex to 71,947 (-2.22%). NOP rally reversed within 4 hours: rupee opened 93.56, hit record low 95.22, closed ~95.07. FPI outflows hit record ₹1.13-1.14 lakh crore in March ($14.8B). Indian worker killed in Iranian strike on Kuwait desalination plant — 7th Indian death in the conflict. Kuwait death expands the geography of Indian casualties (Oman, Saudi Arabia, maritime, now Kuwait). Government H1 FY27 borrowing plan at ₹8.21 lakh crore — lower than expected, keeping bond yields stable at 6.94% despite chaos. India entered FY26 as world's fastest-growing major economy; ended it with record FPI outflows, record-low rupee, Nifty -13.9% from peak. (Sources: Business Standard, NSDL, India TV, Outlook India, Trading Economics)

MORNING UPDATE: Markets CLOSED for Mahavir Jayanti (March 31). FY27 begins April 1 (settlement holiday — trading but no clearing). GIFT Nifty down 1.84% — bearish signal for reopening. India 10Y yield breached 7.03%, biggest monthly surge since Feb 2017 (+37bps in March). USD/INR reference rate at ₹94.15 (tradingeconomics). H1 FY27 government borrowing at ₹8.21L crore — lower than expected, keeping bond yields from further spiking. 7th Indian death confirmed (Kuwait desalination plant). Indian Embassy in Kuwait offering assistance. FY26 ended with: record FPI outflows (₹1.14L crore March), record-low rupee (95.22 intraday), Nifty -13.9% from peak. Bernstein warns: Nifty below 20,000 and rupee beyond ₹110 if conflict persists.

FY26 ended as worst fiscal year since COVID (FY20). GIFT Nifty recovered from morning's -1.84% to +0.16% — extreme overnight pessimism faded. Wide intraday range (22,293-23,679). RBI in holding pattern — no new action, repo 5.25%. Banks pushing back on NOP cap (requesting 3 months vs April 10 deadline). No RBI response. OECD flags possible rate hike Q2. Forex reserves $698.35B (March 20). Bloomberg: $30-40B positions need squaring under NOP cap (higher than earlier $10-18B estimate). SPR 64% full — government NOT considering release. 426,000 evacuees returned from West Asia. FY27 GDP forecasts: S&P 7.1%, OECD 6.1%, Bernstein 2-3% worst case. ICRA warned crude could 'derail' FY27 fiscal deficit target. CONFIRMED.

FY27 Day 1 (April 1): India joined global relief rally but MASSIVELY UNDERPERFORMED — Nifty +1.6% to 22,679, Sensex +1.6% to 73,134, Bank Nifty +2.3% vs KOSPI +8.4%, Nikkei +5.2%, S&P +2.9%. Brent crashed -14% to $101.76 (largest drop since war began) — partial relief for India's import bill but oil still above $100. Three-layer problem persists: (1) Crisis-reversible ~65%: Brent at $101.76, FPI record outflows ₹1.14 lakh crore March, INR at Rs93.43 (yfinance). (2) Structural ~25%: earnings slowdown, record FY27 borrowing ₹17.2T, HDFC NIM compression, FY26 worst: -13.9% Nifty from peak. (3) Regulatory ~10%: NOP cap forcing $30-40B unwinding (April 10), STT hiked (futures 0.02%→0.05%, options 0.10%→0.15%). India VIX 27.89. 200-day EMA at 22,000 = 3.1% below at 22,679. India 10Y breached 7.00% (first since July 2024). RBI granted 3-month extension on capital market exposure norms but NOP cap firm. Urea shortfall ~2M tonnes by August, 60%+ imports from Gulf. Even with ceasefire + $85 oil, only recovers ~65% of losses. (Sources: 5paisa, Business Standard, Bloomberg, NSDL, TradingEconomics, yfinance)

Brent crashed to $101.97 (from $118.35) easing India's import bill — India imports 85-90% of crude. Some rupee relief: INR improved to 92.64. BUT India is the UNDERPERFORMER — Nifty +1.6-1.72% vs KOSPI +8.44%, S&P +2.9%. Three-layer damage persists: crisis-reversible (~65%: oil, FPI, rupee), structural (~25%: earnings miss, record borrowing Rs 17.2T, banking NIM compression), regulatory (~10%: NOP cap, STT hike). Even with full ceasefire + $85 oil, India recovers only ~65% of losses. (Sources: Business Standard, TradingEconomics)

FPI outflows Rs 1.8 trillion in FY26 — worst since 1992, 42% YoY increase. Financial services bore the brunt. Elara Securities: "India is the epicentre of EM outflows." Structural reallocation underway — FPIs won't reverse until oil below $85 AND rupee stabilizes AND earnings trough visible. (Sources: Business Standard, Elara Securities)
India VIX dropped to 25.05 on April 1 (from 27.89) — fear receding but still elevated. Broader markets outperformed: Midcap 100 +3.18%, Smallcap +3.55% on April 1. (Sources: NSE)

RBI MPC meeting scheduled April 6-8 — facing "trade-off central banks dislike most" (imported inflation vs growth slowdown). NOP cap deadline April 10 remains firm — no extension. Banks unwinding $30-40B in forex positions. (Sources: RBI, Business Standard)
India 10Y GOI yield at 7.00-7.04% (+32bps 1M, +50bps YoY) — first breach of 7% since July 2024. Record FY27 borrowing Rs 17.2T is a headwind. (Sources: TradingEconomics, Business Standard)

April 2: Nifty -2.0% to 22,217, Sensex -2.0% to 71,657, Bank Nifty -2.7% to 50,080. Trump speech fallout hit Indian markets — 'hit Iran extremely hard' reversed the April 1 recovery. FPIs net sold ₹8,331 cr on April 1. Rupee in 93-94 range. Oil back at $107 keeps import pressure intense. 200-DMA at 22,000 — only 1% buffer before technical breakdown. RBI MPC April 6-8 is next catalyst — market expects hold but stagflation trap deepening. STT hike effective April 1 adding friction. April 5-8 is the densest event cluster: OPEC+ (Apr 5), energy pause (Apr 6), RBI MPC (Apr 6-8), helium window (Apr 7-10). (Sources: yfinance, BusinessToday, Business Standard)

Bank Nifty barely moved (+0.2% to 51,548) on April 3 — market cautious ahead of RBI MPC April 6-8. Oil at $109-112 devastating for import bill. April 5-8 cluster: OPEC+ (Apr 5), energy pause (Apr 6), RBI MPC (Apr 6-8). Market expects RBI hold at 5.25% but stagflation trap deepening — oil-driven inflation prevents cuts while growth slows. NOP cap April 10 deadline unchanged. 200-DMA at 22,000 remains critical support — 1% buffer. FPIs continuing to sell. Rupee in 92-94 range. (Sources: yfinance, Business Standard, RBI)

CONFIRMED: Rupee surged +1.8% to Rs 93.10/USD on April 4 — largest single-day gain since 2013 — driven by RBI NOP cap forcing banks into massive dollar liquidations. This is REGULATORY FORCE, not organic improvement. Nifty flat (+0.15%) on April 3, range 22,183-22,782. FPIs pulled Rs 19,837 crore in first two April sessions. March outflows record Rs 1.17 lakh crore (NSDL data). Total FY26 outflows Rs 1.8 trillion — worst since 1992. India markets CLOSED April 4 (Good Friday) and April 5 (Saturday). RBI MPC starts April 6-8, expected hold at 5.25%. NOP cap deadline April 10 unchanged. India 10Y at 7.11-7.13% (breached 7%). Monday April 7 absorbs weekend developments — F-15 loss, Kuwait/Bahrain strikes, April 6 deadline outcome — in single session. Bank Nifty -14.8% 1M — worst Indian sector. (Sources: PingTV India, ANI, Business Standard, NSDL, 5paisa, Trading Economics)

RBI MPC Day 1: Six-member committee headed by Governor Sanjay Malhotra convened April 6 for Day 1 of 3-day meeting. Decision announcement April 8. Consensus: HOLD at 5.25% repo rate. Key data entering MPC: Indian crude basket $120.84/bbl average in April (well above all forecast scenarios — CareEdge assumed $90), CPI inflation 3.21% (10-month high) projected 4.5-5.1% for FY27, oil/gas import dependency ~4.2% of GDP. GIFT Nifty at 22,722 (-349.50 points from last close) signals weak Monday April 7 open. FPI outflows: Rs 19,837 crore in 2 April sessions, March record Rs 1.17 lakh crore, FY26 total Rs 1.8 trillion (worst since 1992). NOP deadline April 10: $30-40B in positions still unwinding. NSE/BSE may hold special Saturday trading session April 11 (6-day week). El Nino conditions may weaken monsoons, adding food price risk on top of oil inflation. THREE sequential binaries this week: (1) Tuesday deadline (Apr 7 8 PM ET = Apr 8 5:30 AM IST), (2) RBI MPC decision (Apr 8), (3) NOP cap deadline (Apr 10).

Indian markets rallied on ceasefire hopes after 3-day closure: Nifty +255 (+1.12%) to 22,968, Sensex +787 (+1.07%) to 74,107, Bank Nifty +1,060 (+2.1%) to 52,609. GIFT Nifty 23,048-23,066 suggests flat-to-positive April 7 open. India VIX at 25.52 (elevated but not panic). USD/INR stabilized at ₹93.04 — NOP cap forced liquidation dampening speculative pressure. India 10Y GOI at 7.05% (-5bps, some bond relief). FPI outflows: Rs 23,801 crore in early April, March record Rs 1.18 lakh crore, cumulative 2026 Rs 1.5 trillion. RBI MPC Day 2 today; decision April 8 — consensus HOLD at 5.25%. NOP cap deadline April 10 — $11-15B still unwinding. Special Saturday trading session April 11 confirmed. Oil at $111 = inflation constraint. Relief rally fragile — contingent on Tuesday deadline. Eight Indian LPG tankers have now transited Hormuz under Iran's selective opening. (Sources: Yahoo Finance, Trading Economics, Business Standard, BusinessToday, Bombay Samachar, Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker)

MARKETS STAGED CEASEFIRE-HOPE REBOUND [CONFIRMED] — but front-ran a deal that didn't happen, risking Tuesday unwind. Sensex April 6 close: 74,106.85 (+787.30 / +1.07%); Nifty 50: 22,968.25 (+255.15 / +1.12%); Bank Nifty +2% — day's star. Intraday low: Sensex 72,728.66; Nifty 22,542.95 in early trade (pricing the GIFT Nifty -350 panic signal). Reversal trigger: reports of the US-Iran 45-day ceasefire framework lifted sentiment. Markets bought the Axios story BEFORE Iran rejected it. Indian markets effectively front-ran the ceasefire-hope narrative and may now have to unwind into Tuesday's close on the Iran rejection news. RBI MPC Day 2 deliberations underway April 7; decision Wednesday April 8. Reuters poll: 69 of 71 economists see hold at 5.25%. Rupee at ~Rs 92.93/USD per script (slightly stronger than Bloomberg's earlier 95-100 warning bands). India 10Y GOI eased -5bps to 7.05% on dovish Day 1 read.

EVENING UPDATE: April 6 ceasefire-hope rally REVERSED — Nifty closed ~22,851 (-0.51%), Bank Nifty hardest hit at ~51,549 (-2.0% from 52,609). Session in three acts: crash open (Nifty -129 to 22,838), further cratering (low 22,719), mid-day recovery to 23,006, then fade. India VIX rose to 25.47 (+5.2%). Only 14/50 Nifty stocks green. IT outperformed (+2.06%), financials led losses. FIIs sold Rs 8,167 crore on April 6; DIIs bought Rs 8,000 crore. FII index futures net short -2.68 lakh contracts. Rupee at Rs 93.00-93.07 — flat, caught between NOP support and deadline pressure. India 10Y at 7.05-7.10%. RBI MPC Day 2 underway — BS 10/10 poll, Reuters 69/71, Goodreturns 23/25 for hold at 5.25%. Indian crude basket at $120.84/bbl (46% above RBI $85 assumption). BusinessToday raised rate HIKE question — significant framing shift. NOP cap deadline April 10 firm — $11-15B still unwinding. Special Saturday trading April 11 confirmed. (Sources: Upstox, IBTimes India, 5Paisa, MarketScreener, Business Standard, Reuters)

INDIA TRIPLE BINARY COLLAPSING — RELIEF RALLY ACROSS NIFTY/SENSEX/BANK NIFTY/INR [CONFIRMED]. Day 40 of conflict. Nifty +4.1% to 23,911, Sensex +4.5% to 77,438, Bank Nifty +5.0% to 55,221. INR strengthening from ₹93.01 to ₹92.55. India 10Y at ~7.05% expected to fall 2-5 bps on RBI dovish hold. RBI MPC decision at 10 AM IST today — consensus HOLD at 5.25% but the macro backdrop has been transformed: Brent $94 (vs RBI baseline $85, now within $9 not $25), oil-driven inflation pressure dramatically reduced. BusinessToday's pre-ceasefire 'rate hike question' framing is now dead. NOP cap deadline Friday April 10 — banks awaiting RBI extension decision. Saturday April 11 special session may not be needed. FII net -₹8,336 cr Apr 7 (selling) with DII +₹7,449 cr (buying) — first ceasefire-day FII flip is the next intraday tell.

Nifty 50 closed at 23,997.35 (+3.78%, +873.70 points) on April 8, with intraday high of 24,025 briefly touching 200-DMA at ~24,051. Sensex 77,562.90 (+2,946 / +3.95% — biggest single-day gain in 6 years). Bank Nifty ~55,221 (+5.0%). India VIX crashed 20% to 19.70 — pre-war level. Rs 16.8 lakh crore wealth created in single session. ALL 50 Nifty stocks closed green. Sectoral: Realty +6.76%, Auto +6.55%, PSU Banks +5.79%. April 9 expected cautious/flat per GIFT Nifty. (Source: Upstox, Moolaah, 5paisa, GoodReturns, Bloomberg)

FII flow flip DID NOT happen on ceasefire day. FIIs sold Rs 8,692 crore on April 8 (ceasefire day), continuing selling trend from April 7 (-Rs 8,336 cr). DIIs bought Rs 7,979 crore. Monthly April total: FIIs have sold Rs 37,944 crore. Nifty +3.78% rally driven by SHORT-COVERING + DII buying, NOT FII re-entry. FIIs selling INTO the ceasefire rally. FII flow flip remains the next major tell for rally sustainability. (Source: Trendlyne FII/DII data, NSE FII/DII reports, Upstox)

India 10Y GOI yield fell sharply to 6.91% on April 8 — down 12 bps from ~7.03-7.05%, the biggest single-session move in months and a two-week low. Back below 7% for the first time since early April. The ceasefire + RBI dovish hold pulled yields back ~20 bps from April peak of 7.11-7.13%. (Source: MarketScreener, TradingEconomics)

Indian markets gave back ceasefire rally gains on April 9 as fracture news hit: Nifty 23,775 (-0.9%), Sensex 76,632 (-1.2%), Bank Nifty 54,822 (-1.6%). Oil bouncing +4% to $98.60 restoring CAD pressure. FII selling continued at Rs 8,692 cr — April total Rs 37,944 cr. DII bought Rs 7,979 cr. The rally sustainability question answered: without FII flip, it was a bear market bounce. 200-DMA at ~24,051 rejected on first test. (Sources: yfinance, NSE FII/DII data, Trendlyne)

Day 43. Nifty 23,995 (+0.9%), Sensex 77,166 (+0.7%), Bank Nifty 55,833 (+1.8%) — partial recovery from yesterday's ceasefire reversal. NOP cap compliance deadline TODAY — banks unwound ~75% ($30B of $40B), Malhotra called curbs 'temporary.' Bank Nifty leading = NOP overhang fading. India March CPI confirmed at 3.40% (up from 3.21% in February) — 14-month high but BELOW Reuters consensus of 3.48% (source: PIB April 13, SBI Research April 14). The 'transitory' inflation narrative survives as CPI remains within RBI's 2-6% comfort band. However, composition is alarming: imported inflation hit 6.49% (up from 6.35%), now contributing 43% of overall CPI. Core inflation stable at 3.38%. The crisis-driven import cost channel is the dominant inflation driver and will worsen with sustained $100+ oil. RBI's FY27 inflation projection of 4.6% (set April 8) looks conservative given the blockade. Every Rs 10 fuel price hike adds 20-30 bps to headline CPI.

Markets CLOSED April 14 for Ambedkar Jayanti — 48-hour position lockup. India stated on Hormuz navigation: 'Our ships are moving through Hormuz. We have trade & energy agreements with Iran. We will respect & honour them and expect others not to meddle in our affairs' — a CONFIRMED statement asserting independent navigation policy separate from the US blockade, maintaining India's trade relationship with Iran (source: SCMP, Business Today). State OMCs (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) absorbing Rs 8-10/litre in under-recoveries, holding retail fuel prices steady despite $100+ oil — politically motivated but creates fiscal pressure.

India received FIRST Iranian crude oil since 2019 — two supertankers carrying ~4 million barrels total: Felicity (anchored off Sikka, Gujarat, ~2M bbl) and Jaya (moored near Paradip, Odisha, ~2M bbl). Indian Oil Corporation confirmed purchasing. Enabled by US one-month sanctions waiver for Iranian oil already in transit. CRITICAL: waiver expires April 19 — 5 days from now. If blockade persists AND waiver expires, India loses BOTH traditional Gulf oil (blocked) and newly reopened Iranian supply — double squeeze. India's positioning: 'Our ships are moving through Hormuz. We will respect and honour our agreements with Iran' (CONFIRMED — CNBC, Business Standard, Bloomberg, The Week, OilPrice, Deccan Herald, Tribune India)

~20,000 Indian seafarers stranded on ~2,500 ships west of Strait of Hormuz. India successfully repatriated 8 previously stranded LPG tankers through diplomatic engagement with Iran — vessels crossed Strait safely (CONFIRMED — The News Mill, WION News). India-Iran diplomatic channel functioning for Indian vessels even as US blockade targets Iranian ports. Forex reserves jumped $9B to $697.12B (week ended April 3) — partial recovery from $10.3B drop to $688.06B. But March drop was $30.5B total, down from ATH $728.5B on Feb 27 (day before war). TRIPLE SQUEEZE CONFIRMED. US sanctions waiver for Russian oil purchases EXPIRED April 11 (NEW intelligence — not in previous brief). Iran waiver expires April 19 (4 days). Nifty closed at 24,231 (+1.6%) ABOVE 200-DMA (24,050) for first time since April 10 — first session to price blockade + Russian waiver expiry. (Sources: CNBC, Atlantic Council, BusinessToday, yfinance market data)

FINALISED WAIVER-EVE ROLLUP (India waiver T-1 / expires Sunday April 19 midnight UTC) [CONFIRMED; CORRECTS evening brief's stale-week math]. Indian Oil (2M bbl Iranian, paid in yuan via ICICI Shanghai) + Reliance (Iranian crude to Sikka, Gujarat) raced tankers to deadline; 4-6M bbl confirmed arriving before Sunday, more in transit. Russian waiver already expired April 11; Iranian waiver Sunday midnight UTC. Monday open: both sanctioned-import doors shut simultaneously for first time ever. (Sources: RBI weekly release via IBTimes India/Business Standard, NSDL FPI data via Business Standard/Outlook Business, BusinessToday for Bessent, Business Standard investigative on Reliance/IOC yuan settlement)

INDIA MEA SUMMONS IRANIAN AMBASSADOR FATHALI — FIRST FORMAL INDIA-IRAN FRICTION OF CRISIS [CONFIRMED — 4 independent sources]. Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri summoned Iran's Ambassador Dr Mohammad Fathali Saturday evening IST. Misri conveyed 'deep concern at the shooting incident involving two Indian-flagged ships in the Strait of Hormuz' — specifically naming Sanmar Herald (VLCC, ~2M bbl Iraqi crude, bridge-window damage under direct fire) and Jag Arnav (VLCC, forced westward retreat) — and urged Iran to 'resume at the earliest the process of facilitating India-bound ships across the Strait.' Misri reminded Fathali that Iran had earlier facilitated safe passage of several India-bound ships (reference to pre-April 18 Chabahar-framework de-confliction). Fathali 'undertook to convey these views to Tehran.' India maintained strategic-autonomy silence through March bombing campaign, Khamenei elevation, US blockade announcement, every prior Hormuz escalation; this is the FIRST formal Indian protest of the entire 52-day crisis. Framing deliberately narrow: 'deep concern' (not 'condemns'), urging Iran to resume facilitation (not to stop harassment) — preserves India's lane to restart after the immediate incident. Substantive because (a) India is Iran's largest current oil-customer inside the soon-to-expire US waiver window and (b) Indian-flagged tonnage is a direct MEA-enforceable legal nexus that commercial-vessel incidents do not create. Tehran-side MFA has not commented. HT pro-BJP lean visible — emphasising 'firm' summons and pre-positioning Modi-government-action framing (signal PMO briefing HT). Republic TV / Times Now 'Iran fires on Indian ships' near-propaganda tonal register; content thin. (Sources: The Wire, The Tribune, NewsX, The Federal, Hindustan Times)

WAIVER T-0 + BESSENT CONFIRMATION + RELIANCE REJECTIONS [CONFIRMED]. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent publicly confirmed the general license permitting Iranian oil loaded before March 20 is NOT being renewed: 'We will not be renewing the general license on Russian oil, and we will not be renewing the general license on Iranian oil.' Expiry Monday 05:30 IST / 00:00 UTC April 20. KEY NUANCE: US Treasury issued a SEPARATE 30-day waiver on Russian oil for India (Business Today April 18) — 'no renewals' specifically forecloses Iranian continuity while leaving Russian supply protected (buys India ~30 days to execute supply recalibration toward Iraq/UAE/Saudi/Africa/US/Latin America). GIFT Nifty last 24,186 (April 16 close, no trading Saturday-Sunday). Monday 06:30 IST first GIFT re-pricing; Nifty cash 09:15 reconciles. OMC daily USD demand $250-300M — at Brent $95+ Monday morning becomes binding on spot rupee; USD/INR re-test Rs93+ likely. ET/Business Today outlook filed Friday ('Nifty likely open higher on moderating oil + renewed peace talks') is STALE on Saturday kinetic, SNSC, MEA summons. Retail Monday open may be confused. (Sources: US Treasury/Bessent, Business Today, Bloomberg/Reuters India bureau, Business Standard on Derya/Lenore)

INDIA MEA SUMMONS — IRAN HAS NOT REPLIED PUBLICLY 30+ HOURS LATER [CONFIRMED absence of Iranian MFA statement Sunday]. Iran's Foreign Ministry (Spokesperson Baghaei) has not published an official response to Saturday evening's Misri-Fathali demarche (Sanmar Herald + Jag Arnav). Tehran cannot produce a coherent civilian reply because the IRGC/Araghchi/Pezeshkian triangle is openly fractured. Jerusalem Post citing Iranian state TV reports IRGC-aligned commentators characterising Araghchi as 'idiot' (single-chain framing). The morning brief's india->iran weight-5.0 demarche edge holds with no downgrade justification; the absence of Iranian apology or commitment to resume facilitation IS the update.

INDIAN-DIASPORA GULF EVACUATION POSTURE — NO MEA ADVISORY UPGRADE SUNDAY [CONFIRMED non-event]. No Indian Ministry of External Affairs advisory upgrade from 'caution' to 'avoid non-essential travel' has been published Sunday for Gulf destinations; no Air India wide-body diversion notices; no Indian embassy (Tehran, Abu Dhabi, Doha, Riyadh, Muscat) has announced contact-registration campaigns. Reference point: ~8 million Indians in the Gulf; any evacuation planning would equal severe escalation assessment. The non-arrival of evacuation planning signals India's security establishment is not yet reading Saturday's kinetic as requiring civilian-protection posture. Confirmed by absence across MEA Twitter, Air India route-change notices, embassy websites.

INDIA MEA SUMMONS OF IRAN'S AMBASSADOR FATHALI — 40+ HOURS WITHOUT IRANIAN MFA RESPONSE [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. Saturday 18:00 IST summons of Ambassador Mohammad Fathali by Foreign Secretary Misri has produced no Iranian Foreign Ministry response in 40+ hours at time of writing Monday AM. The Wire, Kashmir Reader, Deccan Chronicle, The Week (India), Open The Magazine continue to carry the summons; no Tehran statement has surfaced. Araghchi's personal X account has addressed Trump and the insurance market but has NOT specifically addressed India or the MEA concern. Fathali "undertook to convey" per MEA readout; no public read-back from Tehran. Load-bearing: Iran cannot produce a coherent civilian response because the civilian wing (Araghchi/Pezeshkian/MFA) is institutionally subordinate to the SNSC's Hormuz ratification, and conceding to India would require rolling back the transit-certificate regime. Touska gives Tehran a way to redirect any eventual response inside a broader US-retaliation posture without singling out India.

BESSENT IRANIAN-OIL WAIVER ELAPSED 05:30 IST MONDAY; NO LAST-MINUTE EXTENSION — RUSSIAN-OIL WAIVER RENEWED (ASYMMETRIC DESIGN) [CONFIRMED ACTION]. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's April 15 public non-renewal declaration ("we will not be renewing the general license on Russian oil, and we will not be renewing the general license on Iranian oil") executed at 05:30 IST Monday on the Iran side — no extension, carve-out, or wind-down. Incremental Iranian supply to Indian refiners structurally zero from 05:30 IST onward. NUANCE versus Friday framing: the Russian waiver DID end up being RENEWED (30-day extension for India) despite Bessent's initial "neither" statement — The Week, Business Today, The Federal all confirm the Russian-side reversal. India's ~1.9 mbpd March Russian flow preserved. This asymmetry — Iranian non-renewal executed, Russian renewal executed — is a deliberate US policy design separating energy supply security (Russian oil continues to cushion global balance, particularly for India) from maximum-pressure sanctions on Iran's regime.

NIFTY MONDAY BASE -0.5 TO -1.0% (24,110-24,230) — REVISED MILDER THAN EVENING'S -1.0 TO -1.5% [REPORTED]. Driver for revision: Russian-oil waiver RENEWED (not in evening framing) preserves ~1.9 mbpd Russian flow to Indian refiners; Brent Monday cash ~$95.42 is $3-4 BELOW Globex $98 that drove evening forecasts; HDFC Bank + ICICI Bank reported Saturday April 18 (read pending). Triple headwind confirmed (not evaporating): Brent ~$95 imported-inflation tail, MEA summons political-nexus friction (40+ hrs no Iran MFA response), Iranian waiver elapsed 05:30 IST (zero marginal Iranian supply). Supportive crosscurrent: Russian-waiver renewal + potential earnings ballast. 200-DMA 24,050 likely holds intraday. OMCs -1.5 to -2.5% on Brent gap; Reliance neutral (refining-margin-beneficiary vs oil-sourcing-complexity penalty).

CUMULATIVE FII OUTFLOW Rs 1.61 LAKH CRORE (~$17.3B AT ₹93.05) SINCE FEB 28 — FULLY ABSORBED BY DOMESTIC MFs [CONFIRMED STRUCTURAL DATA]. Business Today's April 20 sector review confirms the cumulative number; NSDL data consistent. Offsetting flow: domestic mutual funds absorbed essentially the entire FII sell-down via SIP inflows, keeping BSE market capitalization at pre-war levels despite the 8-week kinetic phase. Nifty Monday 24,364.85 (+0.05%) printed FLAT against a backdrop that a-priori predicted -1 to -2% (Brent +5%, Europe -1.1%, US -0.24%, rupee weakening). FLAT print = the signal. The crisis-premium tail the `nifty-50` node contemplated (FII flight → Nifty discount to fundamentals) has been materially muted by domestic-demand absorption. Double-edged for investor thesis: confirms structural resilience (lower left-tail probability) AND raises the hurdle for panic-entry condition (Nifty break of 200-DMA at 24,050). Path to trigger activation now requires compounding event — Wednesday ceasefire lapse + domestic earnings miss + FII acceleration. RBI 5.25% + NDF ban + NOP cap + $3.6B April forex intervention (~$40B per BS column April 19 forex-curb arbitrage unwind) = visible-hand layer. USD/INR ₹93.05 Mon / ₹93.09 Tue = managed print. India 10Y GoI ~6.90-6.95%, state auction Rs 16,900 cr April 21.

FERTILIZER/UREA TRANSMISSION TO INDIA CPI — UREA GRANULAR FOB ME $702/MT SPOT (+81.7% YTD, +69.6% YoY), $830/MT FORWARD [CONFIRMED COMMODITY DATA]. Trading Economics / CME data; pre-war level $400-490/mt. Qatar production halt on urea holding (QatarEnergy confirmed); Saudi/Bahrain/UAE nitrogen supply constrained by conflict. Mechanism: oil/Qatar disruption → urea price surge → fertilizer-subsidy bill (government-absorbed, protecting farmer not CPI directly) + indirect wheat (+2.5%) / corn (+2.4%) input-cost pass-through → food CPI with 1-2 quarter lag. India imports ~25-30% of urea needs. RBI FY27 CPI forecast 4.6% assumes oil-pass-through but may UNDER-WEIGHT fertilizer-pass-through. This is the cleanest India-inflation transmission mechanism not yet priced into the RBI base case. Q2 planting decisions locked in at crisis-adjusted input costs — LOW reversibility.

INDIA IS ABSORBING THE CEASEFIRE EXTENSION AS 'NOT A DEAL' RATHER THAN 'PEACE' — CAPITAL-ACCOUNT STRESS DOMINATING TRADE-ACCOUNT RELIEF [CONFIRMED, multi-source]. Wednesday 11:51 IST: Nifty 50 at 24,411 (-0.7% from Tuesday's 24,576.60 rally close); Sensex 78,706 (-0.7%); Bank Nifty flat; USD/INR ₹93.81 (0.3% weaker vs Tuesday NSE close of ₹93.50). Four simultaneous forces: (1) Indian oil marketers' sticky contracted dollar demand for OPEC May/June lifting; (2) FII continuation; (3) RBI tolerating managed depreciation via NDF ban; (4) broadly weaker Asian FX complex (KRW ₩1,477.68 +0.6%). REGIME READ: rupee has shifted from oil-correlated to capital-flow-correlated. The 21,500 Nifty 200-day EMA trigger now requires compounding events from the capital side, not any single oil shock. Indian IT weakness on US-recession transmission adds second-order indirect-crisis pressure (Infosys/HCL/Wipro). India 10Y G-Sec at 6.90% range-bound with FII debt outflow persisting. signals.content: INDIA-GULF EVACUATION SCALE CRYSTALLISES: 50,000+ INDIAN NATIONALS REPATRIATED SINCE FEB 28 [CONFIRMED multi-source]. Across Qatar, Iran, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, over 50,000 Indian nationals have been repatriated since February 28, 2026. Qatar Airways alone flew 7,600+ Indians home from Doha in 3 days during a peak window. India airlifted 600+ citizens from Iran specifically (April 13). MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal: India's Gulf outreach focused on 'energy security, welfare of the Indian diaspora, and other issues of mutual interest' under PM Modi's direct supervision; 'continue to be in touch with Iranian authorities for the safe passage of our ships through the Strait of Hormuz.' India has 14 Indian-owned/flagged ships still in Persian Gulf as of April 22. Business Today April 24: MEA reissued caution-advisory ('Indian citizens are strongly advised not to...') even as India-Iran flights resumed selectively. The 50,000 figure is significant — ~8M Indians in Gulf as the diaspora baseline; 50K = 0.6% returned, but the rate signals India's security establishment views the situation as severe enough to repatriate at scale. Structurally different from 'watch the situation' posture. (Sources: Dainik Jagran English, The Week, Tribune India, ANI News, India.com, Business Today, VisaHQ)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED multi-source evacuation data + MEA spokesperson direct quotes.

signals.content: INDIAN FUEL-PRICING STRUCTURAL DETERIORATION — OIL FIRMS LOSING ₹20/L PETROL, ₹100/L DIESEL; PUMP PRICES FROZEN ~4 YEARS [CONFIRMED]. Per Business Standard April 23 (cited again April 25) and government statements: state-owned fuel retailers (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) are incurring losses of about ₹20 per litre on petrol and roughly ₹100 per litre on diesel as pump prices remain frozen for nearly four years despite the surge in global oil prices. India crude oil basket stood at $100.41 on Friday April 24 vs February average $69.01. The ₹100/litre diesel loss is the sharpest reading on this — implies the OMC subsidy burden is now genuinely structural, not transitory. Either (a) the government will allow pass-through pricing (politically costly with state elections cycle), (b) absorb the subsidy via OMC equity injection (fiscally costly), or (c) negotiate Iran-rupee oil purchase resumption at sub-market prices via Chabahar (geopolitically costly with US). The fuel-pricing constraint is a hard arithmetic floor on India's diplomatic flexibility — the longer Brent stays elevated, the more India's MEA will be pushed toward the Iran-rupee oil track regardless of US preferences. Petroleum Ministry confirmed losses but said 'no plan to hike price' (election-cycle constraint). The fuel-pricing arithmetic is now the analytical anchor for Indian institutional coverage. (Sources: Business Standard April 23, Government Petroleum Ministry, PPAC basket data)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED government-data sourcing on losses; government statements on pricing freeze.

signals.content: BRANCH 1/2/3 PROTOCOL FOR MONDAY OPEN [PENDING SUNDAY GLOBEX]. Friday close held at Nifty 23,897.95 / Sensex 76,664.21 / USD/INR ₹94.22 / India 10Y 6.98%. Sunday Globex Brent open (03:30 IST Monday) is the binary repricing venue. Three-branch framework: Branch 1 (Brent above $108, ~30-35% prob, structural-break) — Nifty 23,775 likely breaks; USD/INR ₹94.50-94.80 likely; FII outflow regime reactivates; LMT $510-515 entry zone lost. Branch 2 (Brent $103-107, ~45-50% base case, theatrical) — Nifty holds 23,775-24,100; USD/INR ₹94.20-94.40; FII deceleration crystallises into entry phase. Branch 3 (Brent below $103, ~15-20%, positioning) — Nifty rallies toward 24,100; rupee strengthens toward ₹93.80; FII deceleration reverses. India is structurally the worst-positioned major equity market for Branch 1 (import-dependent, FII-dependent, OMC subsidy compounding) and best-positioned for Branch 3 (sustained Brent below $100 reverses CAD pressure, rupee floor reasserts). The structural transition signal: Elara Capital deceleration print + Nifty 23,775 floor holding + RBI April 21 NDF curb reversal = India in stabilisation-into-entry phase from a 3-5 year horizon perspective. Branch 2/3 Monday outcomes lock in deployment window; Branch 1 reopens it but at lower entry level (Nifty 23,500 contrarian).

BRANCH 3 CONFIRMED — BRENT GAPPED -4.6% TO $100.47 SUNDAY GLOBEX / MONDAY ASIA; INDIA CLEANEST BRANCH 3 BENEFICIARY [CONFIRMED]. Brent Sunday Globex gap-down to $100.47 (-4.6%) repriced the entire Branch framework into Branch 3 base case (~60% prob). Mechanism: lower crude → India CAD pressure releases (every $5 Brent move = ~$8B annual import bill change) → rupee structural pressure releases → RBI managed-depreciation regime gets breathing room → FII outflow deceleration (Elara Apr 24: +$106M first positive in 7 weeks) crystallises → Nifty OMC ₹20/L petrol + ₹100/L diesel margin compression unwinds → broader cap-weighted rebound. Monday cash-open probabilities: Nifty 23,897 → 24,000-24,200 area (+0.5-1.5%); USD/INR ₹94.22 → ₹93.80-94.10 strengthening band; India 10Y 6.87% → 6.80-6.85%; IOC/BPCL/HPCL +1.5-3% on diesel margin recovery. FII week of April 28 prints could show +$200-400M weekly inflow if Brent holds sub-$103 sustained. India structurally worst-positioned on Branch 1, best-positioned on Branch 3 — Branch 3 confirmed = stabilisation-into-entry phase active. (Sources: Sunday Globex Brent tape, Elara Capital Apr 24, scripts/market-data.py)

RBI INSTRUCTED OMCs TO USE CRISIS-ERA SPECIAL CREDIT FACILITIES — SECOND CONFIRMED RBI CRISIS-ERA MEASURE OF WAR [CONFIRMED]. Per Reuters via MarketScreener India and ORF expert-speak Sunday-Monday, the RBI has instructed state-run oil refiners (IOC, HPCL, BPCL — controlling ~50% of India's 5.2M bpd refining capacity) to curb spot dollar buying and use special credit facilities for their dollar needs — described as 'crisis-era measures' that have been 'in place for about two weeks' (i.e., activated ~April 13-15). This formalises what yesterday's brief framed as a 'hard arithmetic floor on India's diplomatic flexibility' into actual operational policy. Second confirmed RBI crisis-era measure of war (after cumulative ~$5B+ FX reserve drawdowns earlier April). Branch 3 Brent sub-$103 sustained 2-3 sessions = facility could be wound down (release rupee-positive flow). Government forced choice within 30 days (by 2026-05-27): (a) raise pump prices (politically costly given upcoming state elections); (b) absorb OMC losses via fiscal subsidy (~₹2,800 crore/week); (c) restart Iran-rupee oil track via Chabahar despite US blockade pressure; (d) wait for ceasefire/Brent normalisation (low-prob given Hormuz operational closure). PPAC publishing daily international crude rates — Economic Times read this as a tell that GoI is preparing public for fuel-price pass-through. Union Bank of India: 'every $10/bbl move in oil price significantly widens the current account deficit and pushes inflation higher.' (Sources: Reuters via MarketScreener India, ORF, Business Standard, Livemint Plain Facts, Economic Times, Union Bank of India)

INDIAN PRESS POSTURE — DIPLOMATIC THEATRE IS THEATRE; OPERATIONAL STORY IS FUEL-PRICING ARITHMETIC + RBI SPECIAL-CREDIT-FACILITY + INR ₹94.22 [OBSERVATION]. Business Standard dominated Sunday-Monday by RBI special-credit-facility OMC dollar-curb story. Livemint Plain Facts piece on every $10/bbl Brent move = ~$X-billion-CAD-widening framework. Economic Times picked up PPAC daily international crude rate publication as a tell that GoI is preparing public for fuel-price pass-through. Goodreturns/V3Cars Sunday-Monday fuel prices: petrol ₹94.77/litre Delhi, diesel ₹87.67/litre Delhi, petrol ₹103.54/litre Mumbai. Indian institutional coverage has correctly identified that the diplomatic theatre (cancellation drama, MEA statements) is theatre and the operational story is the fuel-pricing arithmetic + RBI special-credit-facility activation + INR ₹94.22. The press posture confirms institutional convergence on the same analytical anchor (fuel-pricing) as the brief's framing. (Sources: Business Standard, Livemint Plain Facts, Economic Times, Goodreturns, V3Cars)

BRANCH 3 FALSIFIED IN OIL TAPE; CHABAHAR EXIT CLOSES OMC ESCAPE HATCH; NIFTY +0.81% MON ON HORMUZ-FIRST CATALYST [CONFIRMED ACTION]. Multiple developments: (1) yesterday's Brent $100.47 print was a yfinance contract-roll glitch; live tape Mon $108.23 (+2.75%) / Tue $109.39 (+1.08%) — oil reversed UP, not down. India import-bill arithmetic does NOT improve; OMC special-credit-facility regime continues (Day 15-16 of 30-day forced-choice clock). (2) Nifty 50 Mon Apr 27 closed 24,092.70 (+0.81%); Sensex 77,303.63 (+0.83%) — snapped 3-session losing streak. Catalyst was Iran's Hormuz-First proposal (delivered via Pakistan over weekend), NOT Russia-mediator architecture. Tuesday GIFT Nifty signaled negative open at 24,048 (-72 pts) on Trump dissatisfaction. Rally was India-domestic-flow-led; FPIs withdrew ₹48,213 cr from cash equity in first 10 days of April per NSDL (FY26 cumulative ~₹1.04 lakh crore). (3) USD/INR Tue ₹94.17 / RBI ref ₹94.1875 — Day 5 of consecutive declines, -1.3% on week. RBI selling dollars intermittently to slow slide, not defending fixed level. India forex reserves $703B as of Apr 17 (vs $728.5B Feb peak) — cumulative ~$25B drawdown. (4) CHABAHAR EXIT: US sanctions waiver for India's Chabahar Port operation expired April 26; India considering full IPGCFZ divest to Iranian entity, with control reverting to India 'if and when sanctions are lifted' (per WION, TRT World, Logical Indian). India already transferred ~$120M financial commitment. India simultaneously: facing 50% Trump tariffs, negotiating parallel FTA with US, engaging both Washington (for waiver extension) and Tehran (for contingency). The 'Iran-rupee oil track via Chabahar restart' escape hatch is closing — narrows OMC 30-day forced-choice now to: absorb fiscal subsidy / pump-price pass-through / Brent normalization (low-prob). (5) Goldman raised late-2026 Brent forecast to $90 from $80; Goldman estimates Iran has curtailed up to 2.5M bpd. (Sources: Business Standard 'Sensex rises 639 pts; Nifty ends at 24093 on Iran's new peace proposal', Kotak Neo, Udaipur Times, NSDL FPI, DSIJ, Tradebrains, RBI ref rate, WION, Logical Indian, TRT World, Goldman commentary)

signals.content: INDIA RELIEF RALLY ON UAE-OPEC BULLISH-FOR-INDIA NARRATIVE — NIFTY 24,177.65 (+0.76%) BACK INSIDE DEPLOYMENT ZONE; USD/INR DAY 2 OF ₹94.50 BREACH HOLDING [CONFIRMED MARKET DATA]. (1) Nifty 50 closed 24,177.65 (+0.76%, +180 pts), Sensex 77,496.36 (+0.79%, +600+ pts) — back inside deployment zone (24,000-24,200) after Tuesday's 23,995.70 breach. Bank Nifty 55,403.60 FLAT after Tuesday's -1.5%. Composition: (a) UAE-OPEC exit interpreted as bullish for India — Khaleej Times analyst: 'From India's point of view, this is very good development.' UAE actively pursuing bilateral oil supply with India; PSU refiners IOC/BPCL/HPCL already partner ADNOC. Re-rating of energy security narrative — UAE-India bilateral fills part of Iranian/Saudi gap. (b) Q4 FY26 earnings strength — auto/FMCG/realty led; 14/16 Nifty sectors green; Bajaj Finance reporting Wed AM. (c) Bank Nifty FLAT (after -1.5% Tue) — RBI ECL-framework clarification settling banking stress; RBI ECL composite catalyst was specific (regulatory clarification), NOT regime-wide stress. (d) DII buying ₹1,712 Cr Tue offsetting partial FII selling ₹2,103 Cr (DIIs absorbing FII outflows). (2) Counterweights: USD/INR ₹94.73 close, intraday low ₹94.80 — Day 2 of post-₹94.50 breach holding. ₹95.00 round-number trigger ₹0.27 above spot. RBI selective dollar-sales today pared morning weakness from intraday ₹94.80 — RBI cushioning, NOT defending. Brent through $115 intraday — every $1/bbl Brent = ~$1.5B annual India import bill. FII April MTD selling ₹56,364 Cr (very heavy); FY26 cumulative ₹1.75 lakh Cr. Indonesian rupiah record low Wed — first EM-Asia oil-sensitive currency to crack since deployment-zone-tested phase; PARALLEL stress, NOT idiosyncratic India weakness. (3) ASSESSMENT: Technical bounce + earnings-driven relief, NOT crisis-resolution signal. Deployment-zone (24,000-24,200) BREACHED Tuesday close 23,995.70; today's 24,177 close back inside zone but only modestly above 24,000 floor. Close above 24,200 holding into Thursday confirms re-validation; close below 24,000 confirms breach is structural. Bank Nifty's FLAT print is the cleaner signal — tells us RBI ECL composite catalyst was specific. (4) REVERSIBILITY: Crisis-relief component (~20%) reverses on Brent breaking $120 sustained or USD/INR breaking ₹95.00; earnings component (~80%) does not reverse on crisis news. (5) FOMC TONIGHT 22:30 IST: Powell's framing of energy inflation 'transitory' vs 'structural' is highest-information-density variable for tomorrow's Indian markets. Transitory → DXY weakens → INR catches bid → Indian equities further. Structural → DXY firms → INR weakens → Indian equities pressured. (Sources: Business Standard 'RBI intervention cushions rupee'; 'Iran war completes 2 months: Nifty targets cut amid oil, inflation risks'; Khaleej Times 'UAE OPEC exit India view'; Upstox; MultiBagg; TradingEconomics; Polymarket FOMC)

trigger_points.mechanism: Government forced choice within 30 days driven by ₹100/L diesel + ₹20/L petrol OMC losses + RBI special-credit-facility constraint.
